human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for franchise-specific execution boundaries, source quality, product and pricing control discipline, approved supplier handling, exception coverage, commercialization notes, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to franchise execution work for product, pricing, promotion, order, return, and inventory workflows and does not claim legal, pricing-strategy, supplier-approval, or finance authority.

Authority sources cover franchise-specific FTC and NASAA guidance plus NIST retail pricing controls and GS1 item-data standards, so workflow, terminology, evidence, and failure handling are materially changed by the named industry.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete prerequisites, missing completion evidence, supplier-substitution boundaries, pricing and fee override refusals, inventory mismatches, promo rework, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-source handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/franchise-systems-and-multi-unit-enterprise-support/franchise-operations-specialist/ so franchise operations work remains separated from adjacent compliance, field consulting, pricing, finance, supply-chain, and food-safety lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around supplier approval, pricing and fee authority, legal interpretation, and unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated franchise operations specialist that supports evidence-backed franchise execution, exception handling, and completion control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
